title_guess: Harlech / Llandanwg, Shell Island, Lake Vyrnwy, St. Melangell's

pages.text:
We stopped for tea near a pass before a big slate mine. Llan Bleanow Ffestiniog. Then onto the campsite south of Harlech, Llandanwg village. Glan y Gors campsite. A short walk away is a [?B] chapel, sunk in the dunes, St. Tanwg arrived there in the [?6].
We were quite tired after being with the family.

__Thursday__
   We walk behind the dunes in the salt marshes. They are protecting the marram grass which takes 5 years for a [?big] clump to get established. Lunch - cheese pasties + later tea time ice cream at Y Maes Cafe. Well run. V. busy. V. V. windy in the afternoon, keeping the skylight shut. Families on the sandy beach.

__Friday__
   We left before breakfast to get a space in the carpark, but there was a 2 m baffle, so we are

[parted] on the roadside. We cycled against a strong head wind to Shell Island. The previous day the wind had blown me over while I was doing yoga. We cycled around Shell Island – a few shops + lots of campers + sand dunes. Just as we were about to cycle back along the causeway it was closed for high tide when the water covers the road. So Simon bought food in a super market + we had lunch in a sheltered part of the beach. Then we cycled back to the van [the water was still a bit over road] + drove back via Barmouth then up a v. windy, hairy high valley road to a wild camping place for the night, on Wales second highest pass, Hellfire Pass, between Lake Bala + Lake Vyrnwy.
We wild camped.
__Saturday__
We drove along a v. windy road to Lake Vyrnwy After the dam we stopped for bread, loo paper, the Times + Tea. We drove on along beautiful country

lake Vyrnwy / Llyn Efyrnwy
[photo of Lake Vyrnwy / Llyn Efyrnwy with dam and tower]
roads to a village near St. Melangell's church. We had lunch outdoors at the pub – scampi + Lasagna.

captions: The Shrine, St. Melangell's Church, North Powys, David Bilbey
pages.text:
The Shrine
St. Melangell's
Church
North Powys

David Bilbey

After lunch we went on a walk 2½m to St. Melangell's church, an old pilgrimage site. Her shrine was dismantled in the Reformation.
Parts of it were hidden in the lychgate & other buildings. Now her shrine has been rebuilt.
St. Melangell protected a hare from a huntsman in the [?C].
Lots of quail. Smelly. A quail farm?
We camped at Llan fechain, quite near Welshpool
